title: polypodium appalachianum
intro: Polypodium appalachianum, also known as Appalachian polypody, is a species of fern native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Polypodium appalachianum include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as supporting the immune system and protecting against oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with herbal preparations often used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
